text.title: Instagram Ad Set API Configuration
text.summary: API calls and targeting JSON for creating Instagram-only and multi-platform ad sets.

rule.statement: Set publisher_platforms to ["instagram"] and list only instagram_positions to run exclusively on Instagram without Facebook bleed-over.
rule.rationale: Mixed placement campaigns attribute conversions to the cheapest placement (often Facebook Audience Network), inflating apparent performance while under-serving Instagram.

section.title: Multi-Platform Targeting
section.p: To run Instagram and Facebook simultaneously, include both publishers and their respective position arrays.

example.code: curl -X POST "https://graph.facebook.com/v20.0/act_{ad-account-id}/adsets" \ -H "Authorization: Bearer {access-token}" \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -d '{ "name": "Instagram Feed & Stories - US 18-34", "campaign_id": "{campaign-id}", "billing_event": "IMPRESSIONS", "optimization_goal": "OFFSITE_CONVERSIONS", "daily_budget": 5000, "bid_strategy": "LOWEST_COST_WITHOUT_CAP", "status": "PAUSED", "targeting": { "geo_locations": {"countries": ["US"]}, "age_min": 18, "age_max": 34, "publisher_platforms": ["instagram"], "instagram_positions": ["stream", "story", "explore", "reels"] }, "promoted_object": { "pixel_id": "{pixel-id}", "custom_event_type": "PURCHASE" } }'
example.note: Always set status to PAUSED on creation; activate after creative review.

section.title: Budget Allocation by Placement

rule.statement: Start with equal budgets across placements, then reallocate based on cost-per-result after 7 days minimum data.
rule.rationale: Initial algorithmic distribution is not optimized; 7 days provides enough impressions for statistically meaningful placement comparison.

section.p: Typical starting allocation: Feed 40%, Stories 30%, Reels 20%, Explore 10%.
